Data recorded for the experiment at the European XFEL are available at doi:10.22003/XFEL.EU-DATA-003481-00. Source data are provided with this paper. Further experimental data as well as the simulation data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.

Code availability

Codes generated during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.

Acknowledgements

We acknowledge the European XFEL in Schenefeld, Germany, for provision of the XFEL beamtime at the SCS scientific instrument and would like to thank the staff for their assistance. D.P. acknowledges funding from ’la Caixa’ Foundation fellowship (ID 100010434) and the Spanish Ministry of Industry, Economy and Competitiveness (MINECO), grant no PID2019-109931GB-I00. The ICN2 is funded by the CERCA programme/Generalitat de Catalunya and by the Severo Ochoa Centres of Excellence Programme, funded by the Spanish Research Agency (AEI, CEX2021-001214-S). T.C.A. acknowledges funding from the Heisenberg Resonant Inelastic X-ray Scattering (hRIXS) Consortium. We thank D. Hickin for support with the automation of tr-SHG measurements. We thank A. Reich and J.T. Delitz for the design of mechanical components used in tr-XRD experiments. We are grateful to M. Altarelli for careful reading of the manuscript.

Author contributions

G.Merc., with input from L.P.H., G.C., I.A.V., J.Z. and T.L.L., conceived the experiments. G.C. and I.S., with input from G.Merc., designed the sample. J.M.C.R manufactured the sample and D.P. characterized it with θ𝜃\thetaitalic_θ-2⁢θ2𝜃2\theta2 italic_θ scan, RSM and PFM. L.P.H, D.P., G.N.H., R.C., L.M., M.T., S.G., T.C.A., G.Merz., S.P., J.Sc., Z.Y., I.A.V. and G.Merc. performed tr-XRD experiments. C.C. developed an online analysis tool to visualize tr-XRD data in real time. G.Merc. and L.P.H. performed tr-SHG and tr-refl experiments. J.Sa. performed θ𝜃\thetaitalic_θ-2⁢θ2𝜃2\theta2 italic_θ measurements at different sample temperatures. L.P.H. and G.Merc. analyzed all the data. L.P.H. modeled tr-XRD and tr-SHG data. G.Merc., A.S., K.R. and I.A.V. supervised the project. G.Merc. wrote the manuscript, drafted by L.P.H., and with input from all authors. All authors provided critical feedback and helped shape the research, analysis and manuscript.

Ethics declarations

The authors declare no competing interests.

Supplementary Information for:
Ultrafast decoupling of polarization and strain in ferroelectric BaTiO3subscriptBaTiO3\mathrm{BaTiO_{3}}roman_BaTiO start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT

Supplementary Note 1 Sample characterization

Supplementary Note 2 Fit function of tr-XRD, tr-SHG and tr-refl delay scans

The function used to fit the delay scans presented in this work is:

F⁢(t)=h⁢(t)∗g⁢(t).𝐹𝑡∗ℎ𝑡𝑔𝑡F(t)=h(t)\ast g(t).italic_F ( italic_t ) = italic_h ( italic_t ) ∗ italic_g ( italic_t ) . (S1)

Equation (S1) is the convolution of the sum of three exponential decays h⁢(t)=∑i=13Ai⁢exp⁡(−(t−t0)/τi)ℎ𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑖13subscript𝐴𝑖𝑡subscript𝑡0subscript𝜏𝑖h(t)=\sum_{i=1}^{3}A_{i}\exp\big{(}-(t-t_{0})/\tau_{i}\big{)}italic_h ( italic_t ) = ∑ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_exp ( - ( italic_t - italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) / italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ), with the Gaussian function g⁢(t)=2/(σ⁢π)⁢exp⁡(−(t−t0)2/(2⁢σ2))𝑔𝑡2𝜎𝜋superscript𝑡subscript𝑡022superscript𝜎2g(t)=\sqrt{2}/(\sigma\sqrt{\pi})\exp\big{(}-(t-t_{0})^{2}/(2\sigma^{2})\big{)}italic_g ( italic_t ) = square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG / ( italic_σ square-root start_ARG italic_π end_ARG ) roman_exp ( - ( italic_t - italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT / ( 2 italic_σ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ), where σ=Δ⁢t/(2⁢2⁢ln⁡2)𝜎Δ𝑡222\sigma=\Delta t/(2\sqrt{2\ln{2}})italic_σ = roman_Δ italic_t / ( 2 square-root start_ARG 2 roman_ln 2 end_ARG ) and Δ⁢tΔ𝑡\Delta troman_Δ italic_t is the time resolution (FWHM) of our experiment (Supplementary Note 4). The resulting fit function can be written as:

F⁢(t)=∑i=13Ai⁢exp⁡(σ22⁢τi2−t−t0τi)⁢(1−erf⁡(σ2−τi⁢(t−t0)2⁢σ⁢τi)),𝐹𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑖13subscript𝐴𝑖superscript𝜎22superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑖2𝑡subscript𝑡0subscript𝜏𝑖1erfsuperscript𝜎2subscript𝜏𝑖𝑡subscript𝑡02𝜎subscript𝜏𝑖F(t)=\sum_{i=1}^{3}A_{i}\exp\left(\frac{\sigma^{2}}{2\tau_{i}^{2}}-\frac{t-t_{% 0}}{\tau_{i}}\right)\left(1-\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{\sigma^{2}-\tau_{i}(% t-t_{0})}{\sqrt{2}\sigma\tau_{i}}\right)\right),italic_F ( italic_t ) = ∑ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_exp ( divide start_ARG italic_σ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG 2 italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG - divide start_ARG italic_t - italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ) ( 1 - roman_erf ( divide start_ARG italic_σ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT - italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_t - italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_σ italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ) ) , (S2)

with the time constants t0subscript𝑡0t_{0}italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, τ1subscript𝜏1\tau_{1}italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, τ2subscript𝜏2\tau_{2}italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and τ3subscript𝜏3\tau_{3}italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and the amplitudes A1subscript𝐴1A_{1}italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, A2subscript𝐴2A_{2}italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and A3subscript𝐴3A_{3}italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT as fit parameters.

Supplementary Note 3 XRD data

Supplementary Note 4 Bunch arrival time monitor and time resolution of tr-XRD experiments

The bunch arrival time monitor (BAM) at the European XFEL tracks the arrival time of electron bunches in each pulse train, providing invaluable information to improve the time resolution of pump-probe experiments [62, 63]. The BAM measures the electron bunch arrival time with respect to the master clock that is also used to synchronize the pump laser. We employ the most downstream BAM, located at the end of the accelerator tunnel (1932 mtimes1932meter1932\text{\,}\mathrm{m}start_ARG 1932 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_m end_ARG from the laser gun) and about 1.5 kmtimes1.5kilometer1.5\text{\,}\mathrm{km}start_ARG 1.5 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_km end_ARG upstream of the interaction point at the sample position. In previous time-resolved experiments at the SCS Instrument, it has been observed that the time drifts measured by the BAM are amplified by 17%−45%percent17percent4517\%-45\%17 % - 45 % at the interaction point [64]. In practice, this translates in a further correction of up to few tens of femtoseconds. In our experiments, the exact amplification coefficient is not known, thus we assume a direct proportionality between BAM and t0subscript𝑡0t_{0}italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT at the experiment, where t0subscript𝑡0t_{0}italic_t start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT indicates the temporal overlap between XFEL and optical laser. This assumption is justified by the dynamics of tr-XRD experiments taking place on the picosecond timescale (Figure 1).

We measure a standard deviation of the BAM within a pulse train of 10 fstimes10femtosecond10\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG, and a standard deviation of the BAM from train to train of 84 fstimes84femtosecond84\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}start_ARG 84 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG. Most importantly, slow drifts of the arrival time of FEL pulses over the course of hours are present and shown in Figure S10 for each recorded run. This indicates that without BAM correction, the time resolution of our experiments might be limited up to ≈600 fsabsenttimes600femtosecond\approx$600\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$≈ start_ARG 600 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG, if we average non-consecutive runs. To improve our time resolution, the time delays of our data, indicating the relative time between FEL and optical laser pulses, are corrected for each pulse train according to the average BAM value of the respective pulse train. Thus, the resulting expected time resolution is Δ⁢t=τFEL2+στFEL2+τOL2+στOL2≈90 fsΔ𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜏FEL2superscriptsubscript𝜎subscript𝜏FEL2superscriptsubscript𝜏OL2superscriptsubscript𝜎subscript𝜏OL2times90femtosecond\Delta t=\sqrt{\tau_{\mathrm{FEL}}^{2}+\sigma_{\tau_{\mathrm{FEL}}}^{2}+\tau_{% \mathrm{OL}}^{2}+\sigma_{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}^{2}}\approx$90\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$roman_Δ italic_t = square-root start_ARG italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_FEL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_σ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_FEL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_σ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG ≈ start_ARG 90 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG. This assumes an FEL pulse duration of τFEL≈35 fssubscript𝜏FELtimes35femtosecond\tau_{\mathrm{FEL}}\approx$35\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_FEL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ≈ start_ARG 35 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG, including the nominal 25 fstimes25femtosecond25\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}start_ARG 25 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG pulse duration [65] and the pulse stretching at the monochromator of ≈10 fsabsenttimes10femtosecond\approx$10\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$≈ start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG (FWHM) [66], a pulse-to-pulse jitter in the train of στFEL≈20 fssubscript𝜎subscript𝜏FELtimes20femtosecond\sigma_{\tau_{\mathrm{FEL}}}\approx$20\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$italic_σ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_FEL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ≈ start_ARG 20 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG, an optical laser pulse duration of τOL≈70 fssubscript𝜏OLtimes70femtosecond\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}\approx$70\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ≈ start_ARG 70 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG and temporal jitter of στOL≈30 fssubscript𝜎subscript𝜏OLtimes30femtosecond\sigma_{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}\approx$30\text{\,}\mathrm{fs}$italic_σ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ≈ start_ARG 30 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_fs end_ARG.

Supplementary Note 5 SHG and reflectivity data

Supplementary Note 6 Sample properties

Supplementary Note 6.1 Transmittance profile of 266 nmtimes266nanometer266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG beam in BTO/SRO/GSO

The transmittance of the 266 nmtimes266nanometer266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG beam in our BTO/SRO/GSO sample, displayed in Figure S16, is calculated as:

𝒯BTO⁢(z)=exp⁡(−z/(δBTO⁢cos⁡θtBTO)),for⁢  0<z<dBTO𝒯SRO⁢(z)=𝒯BTO⁢(dBTO)⁢(1−RpSRO)⁢exp⁡(−z/(δSRO⁢cos⁡θtSRO)),for⁢dBTO<z<dBTO+dSRO𝒯GSO⁢(z)=𝒯SRO⁢(dBTO+dSRO)⁢(1−RpGSO)⁢exp⁡(−z/(δGSO⁢cos⁡θtGSO)),for⁢z>dBTO+dSRO.formulae-sequenceformulae-sequencesubscript𝒯BTO𝑧𝑧subscript𝛿BTOsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡BTOfor  0𝑧subscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝒯SRO𝑧subscript𝒯BTOsubscript𝑑BTO1superscriptsubscript𝑅𝑝SRO𝑧subscript𝛿SROsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡SROforsubscript𝑑BTO𝑧subscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝑑SROsubscript𝒯GSO𝑧subscript𝒯SROsubscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝑑SRO1superscriptsubscript𝑅𝑝GSO𝑧subscript𝛿GSOsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡GSOfor𝑧subscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝑑SRO\begin{split}&\mathcal{T}_{\mathrm{BTO}}(z)=\exp(-z/(\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}\cos% {\theta_{t}^{\mathrm{BTO}}})),\mathrm{for}\,\,0<z<d_{\mathrm{BTO}}\\ &\mathcal{T}_{\mathrm{SRO}}(z)=\mathcal{T}_{\mathrm{BTO}}(d_{\mathrm{BTO}})(1-% R_{p}^{\mathrm{SRO}})\exp(-z/(\delta_{\mathrm{SRO}}\cos{\theta_{t}^{\mathrm{% SRO}}})),\mathrm{for}\,\,d_{\mathrm{BTO}}<z<d_{\mathrm{BTO}}+d_{\mathrm{SRO}}% \\ &\mathcal{T}_{\mathrm{GSO}}(z)=\mathcal{T}_{\mathrm{SRO}}(d_{\mathrm{BTO}}+d_{% \mathrm{SRO}})(1-R_{p}^{\mathrm{GSO}})\exp(-z/(\delta_{\mathrm{GSO}}\cos{% \theta_{t}^{\mathrm{GSO}}})),\mathrm{for}\,\,z>d_{\mathrm{BTO}}+d_{\mathrm{SRO% }}.\end{split}start_ROW start_CELL end_CELL start_CELL caligraphic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z ) = roman_exp ( - italic_z / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) , roman_for 0 < italic_z < italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_CELL end_ROW start_ROW start_CELL end_CELL start_CELL caligraphic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z ) = caligraphic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ( 1 - italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) roman_exp ( - italic_z / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) , roman_for italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_z < italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_CELL end_ROW start_ROW start_CELL end_CELL start_CELL caligraphic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z ) = caligraphic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ( 1 - italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) roman_exp ( - italic_z / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) , roman_for italic_z > italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT . end_CELL end_ROW (S3)

The penetration depths δBTOsubscript𝛿BTO\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, δSROsubscript𝛿SRO\delta_{\mathrm{SRO}}italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and δGSOsubscript𝛿GSO\delta_{\mathrm{GSO}}italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are calculated as detailed in Supplementary Note 6.2. The reflections at the air-BTO, BTO-SRO, and SRO-GSO interfaces (Table S2) are determined by using Snell’s law n1⁢sin⁡θi=n2⁢sin⁡θtsubscript𝑛1subscript𝜃𝑖subscript𝑛2subscript𝜃𝑡n_{1}\sin{\theta_{i}}=n_{2}\sin{\theta_{t}}italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_sin italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_sin italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and the Fresnel equation for p𝑝pitalic_p-polarized light Rp=|(n1⁢cos⁡θt−n2⁢cos⁡θi)/(n1⁢cos⁡θt+n2⁢cos⁡θi)|2subscript𝑅𝑝superscriptsubscript𝑛1subscript𝜃𝑡subscript𝑛2subscript𝜃𝑖subscript𝑛1subscript𝜃𝑡subscript𝑛2subscript𝜃𝑖2R_{p}=|(n_{1}\cos{\theta_{t}}-n_{2}\cos{\theta_{i}})/(n_{1}\cos{\theta_{t}}+n_% {2}\cos{\theta_{i}})|^{2}italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = | ( italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) / ( italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) | start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Here, n1subscript𝑛1n_{1}italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and n2subscript𝑛2n_{2}italic_n start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are the real part of n∗superscript𝑛n^{*}italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT at the two sides of the interface, while θisubscript𝜃𝑖\theta_{i}italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and θtsubscript𝜃𝑡\theta_{t}italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are the angles of the incident and the transmitted beam with respect to the surface normal, with θiBTO=4⁢°superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑖BTO4°\theta_{i}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=4\degreeitalic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = 4 °. The index of refraction of SRO and GSO are calculated from Ref. [68], as detailed in Supplementary Note 6.2, while the index of refraction of the BTO thin film is determined from ellipsometry data at 266 nmtimes266nanometer266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG in Ref. [28].

Given the parameters above (summarized in Table S2), the absorbed fluences in the BTO and SRO thin films are calculated as:

FabsBTO=Fin⁢(1−RpBTO)⁢(1−exp⁡(−dBTO/(δBTO⁢cos⁡θtBTO)))=0.7⁢Finsuperscriptsubscript𝐹absBTOsubscript𝐹in1superscriptsubscript𝑅𝑝BTO1subscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝛿BTOsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡BTO0.7subscript𝐹inF_{\mathrm{abs}}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=F_{\mathrm{in}}(1-R_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}})(1-\exp% (-d_{\mathrm{BTO}}/(\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}\cos{\theta_{t}^{\mathrm{BTO}}})))=0.% 7F_{\mathrm{in}}italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_in end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( 1 - italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ( 1 - roman_exp ( - italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) ) = 0.7 italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_in end_POSTSUBSCRIPT (S4)
FabsSRO=Fin⁢(1−RpBTO)⁢exp⁡[−dBTO/(δBTO⁢cos⁡θtBTO)]⁢(1−RpSRO)⁢[1−exp⁡(−dSRO/(δSRO⁢cos⁡θtSRO))]=0.12⁢Fin,superscriptsubscript𝐹absSROsubscript𝐹in1superscriptsubscript𝑅𝑝BTOsubscript𝑑BTOsubscript𝛿BTOsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡BTO1superscriptsubscript𝑅𝑝SROdelimited-[]1subscript𝑑SROsubscript𝛿SROsuperscriptsubscript𝜃𝑡SRO0.12subscript𝐹inF_{\mathrm{abs}}^{\mathrm{SRO}}=F_{\mathrm{in}}(1-R_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}})\exp[-d% _{\mathrm{BTO}}/(\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}\cos{\theta_{t}^{\mathrm{BTO}}})](1-R_{p% }^{\mathrm{SRO}})[1-\exp(-d_{\mathrm{SRO}}/(\delta_{\mathrm{SRO}}\cos{\theta_{% t}^{\mathrm{SRO}}}))]=0.12F_{\mathrm{in}},italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_in end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( 1 - italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) roman_exp [ - italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ] ( 1 - italic_R start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) [ 1 - roman_exp ( - italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) ] = 0.12 italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_in end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , (S5)

and are reported in Table S1.

Supplementary Note 6.2 Penetration depths

The absorption coefficient α𝛼\alphaitalic_α of the 266 nmtimes266nanometer266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG pump laser in our BTO thin film is determined based on the study of α𝛼\alphaitalic_α as a function of strain in Ref. [28]. For a compressive strain of −0.55%percent0.55-0.55\%- 0.55 %, the penetration depth is δBTO=1/αBTO=17.9 nmsubscript𝛿BTO1subscript𝛼BTOtimes17.9nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}=1/\alpha_{\mathrm{BTO}}=$17.9\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 1 / italic_α start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 17.9 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG. The penetration depths in the SRO thin film δSRO=21.5 nmsubscript𝛿SROtimes21.5nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{SRO}}=$21.5\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 21.5 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG and in the GSO substrate δGSO=26.1 nmsubscript𝛿GSOtimes26.1nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{GSO}}=$26.1\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_GSO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 26.1 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG are determined from the respective dielectric constants ϵ1subscriptitalic-ϵ1\epsilon_{1}italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and ϵ2subscriptitalic-ϵ2\epsilon_{2}italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, reported in Ref. [68]. Specifically, δ=1/α=λ/(4⁢π⁢k)𝛿1𝛼𝜆4𝜋𝑘\delta=1/\alpha=\lambda/(4\pi k)italic_δ = 1 / italic_α = italic_λ / ( 4 italic_π italic_k ), where λ=266 nm𝜆times266nanometer\lambda=$266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_λ = start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG is the wavelength of the pump laser, and k𝑘kitalic_k is the imaginary part of the complex index of refraction n∗=n+i⁢ksuperscript𝑛𝑛𝑖𝑘n^{*}=n+ikitalic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_n + italic_i italic_k, with n=(|ϵ∗|+ϵ1)/2𝑛superscriptitalic-ϵsubscriptitalic-ϵ12n=\sqrt{(|\epsilon^{*}|+\epsilon_{1})/2}italic_n = square-root start_ARG ( | italic_ϵ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) / 2 end_ARG, k=(|ϵ∗|−ϵ1)/2𝑘superscriptitalic-ϵsubscriptitalic-ϵ12k=\sqrt{(|\epsilon^{*}|-\epsilon_{1})/2}italic_k = square-root start_ARG ( | italic_ϵ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | - italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) / 2 end_ARG, and |ϵ∗|=ϵ12+ϵ22superscriptitalic-ϵsuperscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ12superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϵ22|\epsilon^{*}|=\sqrt{\epsilon_{1}^{2}+\epsilon_{2}^{2}}| italic_ϵ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = square-root start_ARG italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG. The penetration depth of 800 nmtimes800nanometer800\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 800 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG and 400 nmtimes400nanometer400\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 400 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG in BTO, δBTO800 nm=944 nmsuperscriptsubscript𝛿BTOtimes800nanometertimes944nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}^{$800\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$}=$944\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_ARG 800 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG 944 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG and δBTO400 nm=167 nmsuperscriptsubscript𝛿BTOtimes400nanometertimes167nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}^{$400\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$}=$167\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_ARG 400 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG 167 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG, are obtained from the equations above, calculating the complex dielectric function as ϵ∗=ϵ1+i⁢ϵ2=sin⁡θi2⁢[1+tan⁡θi2⁢(1−ρ1+ρ)2]superscriptitalic-ϵsubscriptitalic-ϵ1𝑖subscriptitalic-ϵ2superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑖2delimited-[]1superscriptsubscript𝜃𝑖2superscript1𝜌1𝜌2\epsilon^{*}=\epsilon_{1}+i\epsilon_{2}=\sin{\theta_{i}}^{2}[1+\tan{\theta_{i}% }^{2}(\frac{1-\rho}{1+\rho})^{2}]italic_ϵ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_i italic_ϵ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = roman_sin italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ 1 + roman_tan italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( divide start_ARG 1 - italic_ρ end_ARG start_ARG 1 + italic_ρ end_ARG ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], where ρ=tan⁡Ψ⁢exp⁡(i⁢Δ)𝜌Ψ𝑖Δ\rho=\tan{\Psi}\exp(i\Delta)italic_ρ = roman_tan roman_Ψ roman_exp ( italic_i roman_Δ ), with ΨΨ\Psiroman_Ψ and ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ reported in Ref. [28]. Due to the large penetration depths δBTO800 nmsuperscriptsubscript𝛿BTOtimes800nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}^{$800\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$}italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_ARG 800 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and δBTO400 nmsuperscriptsubscript𝛿BTOtimes400nanometer\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}^{$400\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$}italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_ARG 400 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, we probe the entire BTO thickness (dBTO=34.5 nmsubscript𝑑BTOtimes34.5nanometerd_{\mathrm{BTO}}=$34.5\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}$italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 34.5 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG).

Supplementary Note 7 Two-temperature model

Electron and phonon temperatures, Te⁢(z,t)subscript𝑇𝑒𝑧𝑡T_{e}(z,t)italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ) and Tp⁢(z,t)subscript𝑇𝑝𝑧𝑡T_{p}(z,t)italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ), result from the analytical solution of the two-temperature model (2TM), consisting of the following coupled equations [74]:

Ce⁢∂Te∂tsubscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝑇𝑒𝑡\displaystyle C_{e}\frac{\partial T_{e}}{\partial t}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT divide start_ARG ∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG ∂ italic_t end_ARG =−g⁢(Te−Tp)+S⁢(z,t),absent𝑔subscript𝑇𝑒subscript𝑇𝑝𝑆𝑧𝑡\displaystyle=-g(T_{e}-T_{p})+S(z,t),= - italic_g ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) + italic_S ( italic_z , italic_t ) , (S6)
Cp⁢∂Tp∂tsubscript𝐶𝑝subscript𝑇𝑝𝑡\displaystyle C_{p}\frac{\partial T_{p}}{\partial t}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT divide start_ARG ∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG ∂ italic_t end_ARG =g⁢(Te−Tp),absent𝑔subscript𝑇𝑒subscript𝑇𝑝\displaystyle=g(T_{e}-T_{p}),= italic_g ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT - italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ,

where Cesubscript𝐶𝑒C_{e}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Cpsubscript𝐶𝑝C_{p}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are volumetric heat capacities, respectively, and g𝑔gitalic_g is the electron-phonon coupling. The source term S⁢(z,t)𝑆𝑧𝑡S(z,t)italic_S ( italic_z , italic_t ) represents the absorbed power density ( W m−3timesabsenttimeswattmeter3\text{\,}\mathrm{W}\text{\,}{\mathrm{m}}^{-3}start_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_W end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_m end_ARG start_ARG - 3 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG) of the pump laser in our sample as a function of t𝑡titalic_t and z𝑧zitalic_z. It is defined as S⁢(z,t)=Fabs⁢exp⁡(−z/(δ⁢cos⁡θt))⁢exp⁡(−t2/(2⁢τOL2))/(δ⁢τOL)𝑆𝑧𝑡subscript𝐹abs𝑧𝛿subscript𝜃𝑡superscript𝑡22superscriptsubscript𝜏OL2𝛿subscript𝜏OLS(z,t)=F_{\mathrm{abs}}\exp{(-z/(\delta\cos{\theta_{t}}))}\exp{(-t^{2}/(2\tau_% {\mathrm{OL}}^{2}))}/(\delta\tau_{\mathrm{OL}})italic_S ( italic_z , italic_t ) = italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_exp ( - italic_z / ( italic_δ roman_cos italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ) roman_exp ( - italic_t start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT / ( 2 italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ) / ( italic_δ italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ), where Fabssubscript𝐹absF_{\mathrm{abs}}italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the pump absorbed fluence (Supplementary Note 6.1), δ𝛿\deltaitalic_δ is the penetration depth (Supplementary Note 6.2), θtsubscript𝜃𝑡\theta_{t}italic_θ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_t end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the angle of the transmitted optical beam with respect to the surface normal, and τOLsubscript𝜏OL\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the optical laser pulse duration (Supplementary Note 4).

In general, equation (S6) contains also the diffusion terms Ke⁢∂2Te/∂z2subscript𝐾𝑒superscript2subscript𝑇𝑒superscript𝑧2K_{e}\partial^{2}T_{e}/\partial z^{2}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∂ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ∂ italic_z start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and Kp⁢∂2Tp/∂z2subscript𝐾𝑝superscript2subscript𝑇𝑝superscript𝑧2K_{p}\partial^{2}T_{p}/\partial z^{2}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∂ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ∂ italic_z start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, where Kesubscript𝐾𝑒K_{e}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Kpsubscript𝐾𝑝K_{p}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are carrier and thermal conductivity, respectively. In the particular case of BTO, carrier and thermal diffusion terms can be neglected because the parameters De/(ν⁢δBTO)=2.5×10−5 subscript𝐷𝑒𝜈subscript𝛿BTOtimes2.5E-5absentD_{e}/(\nu\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}})=$2.5\text{\times}{10}^{-5}\text{\,}$italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ( italic_ν italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = start_ARG start_ARG 2.5 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG - 5 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG end_ARG and Dp/(ν⁢δBTO)=1.1×10−2 subscript𝐷𝑝𝜈subscript𝛿BTOtimes1.1E-2absentD_{p}/(\nu\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}})=$1.1\text{\times}{10}^{-2}\text{\,}$italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ( italic_ν italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = start_ARG start_ARG 1.1 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG - 2 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG end_ARG are relatively small. Here, the electron diffusion coefficient is calculated from the Einstein relation De=μ⁢kB⁢T/q=2.6×10−7 m2 s−1subscript𝐷𝑒𝜇subscript𝑘𝐵𝑇𝑞times2.6E-7timesmeter2second1D_{e}=\mu k_{B}T/q=$2.6\text{\times}{10}^{-7}\text{\,}{\mathrm{m}}^{2}\text{\,% }{\mathrm{s}}^{-1}$italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_μ italic_k start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_B end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_T / italic_q = start_ARG start_ARG 2.6 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG - 7 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_m end_ARG start_ARG 2 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_s end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG, where μ=0.1 cm2 V−1 s−1𝜇times0.1timescentimeter2volt1second1\mu=$0.1\text{\,}{\mathrm{cm}}^{2}\text{\,}{\mathrm{V}}^{-1}\text{\,}{\mathrm{% s}}^{-1}$italic_μ = start_ARG 0.1 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_cm end_ARG start_ARG 2 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_V end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_s end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG is the BTO electron mobility [75], kBsubscript𝑘𝐵k_{B}italic_k start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_B end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the Boltzmann constant, T𝑇Titalic_T is the sample temperature and q𝑞qitalic_q is the electron charge, while the BTO thermal diffusivity [73] is Dp=1.11 mm2 s−1subscript𝐷𝑝times1.11timesmillimeter2second1D_{p}=$1.11\text{\,}{\mathrm{mm}}^{2}\text{\,}{\mathrm{s}}^{-1}$italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 1.11 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_mm end_ARG start_ARG 2 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_s end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG.

We turn now to discuss the volumetric heat capacities Cesubscript𝐶𝑒C_{e}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Cpsubscript𝐶𝑝C_{p}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Electron and phonon volumetric heat capacities of SRO and GSO are taken from Refs. [69, 70], assuming T=300 K𝑇times300kelvinT=$300\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$italic_T = start_ARG 300 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG. This assumption is justified by the fact that in GSO the transmittance of the 266 nmtimes266nanometer266\text{\,}\mathrm{nm}start_ARG 266 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_nm end_ARG beam is essentially 0%percent00\%0 %, while in the SRO thin film only 12%percent1212\%12 % of the incident fluence is absorbed. For the absorbed fluence Fabs=0.33 mJ cm−2subscript𝐹abstimes0.33timesmillijoulecentimeter2F_{\mathrm{abs}}=$0.33\text{\,}\mathrm{mJ}\text{\,}{\mathrm{cm}}^{-2}$italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 0.33 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_mJ end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_cm end_ARG start_ARG - 2 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG (Supplementary Note 6.1), the maximum temperature increase [41] in SRO is Δ⁢T=FabsSRO/(δSRO⁢CpSRO)=55 KΔ𝑇superscriptsubscript𝐹absSROsubscript𝛿SROsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝SROtimes55kelvin\Delta T=F_{\mathrm{abs}}^{\mathrm{SRO}}/(\delta_{\mathrm{SRO}}C_{p}^{\mathrm{% SRO}})=$55\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$roman_Δ italic_T = italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) = start_ARG 55 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG, with a consequent increase of CpSROsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝SROC_{p}^{\mathrm{SRO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT of only 2%percent22\%2 %. Conversely, CeSROsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑒SROC_{e}^{\mathrm{SRO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT increases by 18%percent1818\%18 %, but the absolute value remains one order of magnitude smaller than CpSROsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝SROC_{p}^{\mathrm{SRO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_SRO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. The volumetric heat capacity CpBTOsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝BTOC_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT as a function of T𝑇Titalic_T is reported in Ref. [76]. Given the weak temperature dependence for T>300 K𝑇times300kelvinT>$300\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$italic_T > start_ARG 300 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG, we consider CpBTO=2.8×106 J m−3 K−1superscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝BTOtimes2.8E6timesjoulemeter3kelvin1C_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=$2.8\text{\times}{10}^{6}\text{\,}\mathrm{J}\text{\,}{% \mathrm{m}}^{-3}\text{\,}{\mathrm{K}}^{-1}$italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG start_ARG 2.8 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG 6 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_J end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_m end_ARG start_ARG - 3 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_K end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG and calculate Δ⁢T=FabsBTO/(δBTO⁢CpBTO)=375 KΔ𝑇superscriptsubscript𝐹absBTOsubscript𝛿BTOsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝BTOtimes375kelvin\Delta T=F_{\mathrm{abs}}^{\mathrm{BTO}}/(\delta_{\mathrm{BTO}}C_{p}^{\mathrm{% BTO}})=$375\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$roman_Δ italic_T = italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT / ( italic_δ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) = start_ARG 375 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG and 196 Ktimes196kelvin196\text{\,}\mathrm{K}start_ARG 196 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG, for FabsBTO=1.88 mJ cm−2superscriptsubscript𝐹absBTOtimes1.88timesmillijoulecentimeter2F_{\mathrm{abs}}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=$1.88\text{\,}\mathrm{mJ}\text{\,}{\mathrm{cm}% }^{-2}$italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG 1.88 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_mJ end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_cm end_ARG start_ARG - 2 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG and 0.98 mJ cm−2times0.98timesmillijoulecentimeter20.98\text{\,}\mathrm{mJ}\text{\,}{\mathrm{cm}}^{-2}start_ARG 0.98 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_mJ end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_cm end_ARG start_ARG - 2 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG (Supplementary Note 6.1), respectively. Due to the weak dependence on T𝑇Titalic_T, the average of CpBTOsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝BTOC_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT between 300 Ktimes300kelvin300\text{\,}\mathrm{K}start_ARG 300 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG and 300 K+Δ⁢Ttimes300kelvinΔ𝑇$300\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$+\Delta Tstart_ARG 300 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG + roman_Δ italic_T yields the self-consistent result of CpBTO=2.8×106 J m−3 K−1superscriptsubscript𝐶𝑝BTOtimes2.8E6timesjoulemeter3kelvin1C_{p}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=$2.8\text{\times}{10}^{6}\text{\,}\mathrm{J}\text{\,}{% \mathrm{m}}^{-3}\text{\,}{\mathrm{K}}^{-1}$italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG start_ARG 2.8 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG 6 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_J end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_m end_ARG start_ARG - 3 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_K end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG [76]. Finally, to estimate CeBTOsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑒BTOC_{e}^{\mathrm{BTO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, we rely on the available data from other titanates, e.g. SrTiO3 [77] and CaTiO3 [78], and observe that they have an average ratio Ce/Cp=0.015subscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝0.015C_{e}/C_{p}=0.015italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 0.015 for the T𝑇Titalic_T ranges considered here. This yields CeBTO=4.2×104 J m−3 K−1superscriptsubscript𝐶𝑒BTOtimes4.2E4timesjoulemeter3kelvin1C_{e}^{\mathrm{BTO}}=$4.2\text{\times}{10}^{4}\text{\,}\mathrm{J}\text{\,}{% \mathrm{m}}^{-3}\text{\,}{\mathrm{K}}^{-1}$italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = start_ARG start_ARG 4.2 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG 10 end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG start_ARG roman_J end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_m end_ARG start_ARG - 3 end_ARG end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG power start_ARG roman_K end_ARG start_ARG - 1 end_ARG end_ARG end_ARG. Although CeBTOsuperscriptsubscript𝐶𝑒BTOC_{e}^{\mathrm{BTO}}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT roman_BTO end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a temperature-dependent quantity, for simplicity, we assume it to be constant here. For completeness, all the physical constants employed in the solution of the 2TM are reported in Table S2.

We focus now on the analytical solution of the 2TM. The second equation of equation (S6) can be written as:

Te=Cpg⁢∂Tp∂t+Tp,subscript𝑇𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝𝑔subscript𝑇𝑝𝑡subscript𝑇𝑝T_{e}=\frac{C_{p}}{g}\frac{\partial T_{p}}{\partial t}+T_{p},italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = divide start_ARG italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG italic_g end_ARG divide start_ARG ∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG ∂ italic_t end_ARG + italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , (S7)

which is then inserted in the first equation of (S6) and yields the following second order differential equation of Tpsubscript𝑇𝑝T_{p}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT:

Ce⁢Cpg⁢∂2Tp∂t2+(Ce+Cp)⁢∂Tp∂t=S⁢(z,t).subscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝𝑔superscript2subscript𝑇𝑝superscript𝑡2subscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝subscript𝑇𝑝𝑡𝑆𝑧𝑡\frac{C_{e}C_{p}}{g}\frac{\partial^{2}T_{p}}{\partial t^{2}}+(C_{e}+C_{p})% \frac{\partial T_{p}}{\partial t}=S(z,t).divide start_ARG italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG italic_g end_ARG divide start_ARG ∂ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG ∂ italic_t start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG + ( italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) divide start_ARG ∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG ∂ italic_t end_ARG = italic_S ( italic_z , italic_t ) . (S8)

Given the following initial and boundary conditions: Te⁢(z,0)=Tp⁢(z,0)=T0=300 Ksubscript𝑇𝑒𝑧0subscript𝑇𝑝𝑧0subscript𝑇0times300kelvinT_{e}(z,0)=T_{p}(z,0)=T_{0}=$300\text{\,}\mathrm{K}$italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , 0 ) = italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , 0 ) = italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = start_ARG 300 end_ARG start_ARG times end_ARG start_ARG roman_K end_ARG, ∂Tp/∂z⁢(0,t)=0subscript𝑇𝑝𝑧0𝑡0\partial T_{p}/\partial z(0,t)=0∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ∂ italic_z ( 0 , italic_t ) = 0, ∂Tp/∂t⁢(z,0)=0subscript𝑇𝑝𝑡𝑧00\partial T_{p}/\partial t(z,0)=0∂ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / ∂ italic_t ( italic_z , 0 ) = 0, we can derive the lattice temperature Tp⁢(z,t)subscript𝑇𝑝𝑧𝑡T_{p}(z,t)italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ) as an analytical solution of equation (S8):

Tp⁢(z,t)=T0+π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢exp⁡(τOL2⁢ℬ22⁢𝒜2)⁢erf⁡(ℬ⁢τOL2⁢A−t2⁢τOL)⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢exp⁡(−ℬ𝒜⁢t)+π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢erf⁡(12⁢τOL⁢t)−π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢exp⁡(τOL2⁢ℬ22⁢𝒜2)⁢erf⁡(ℬ⁢τOL2⁢A)⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢exp⁡(−ℬ𝒜⁢t),subscript𝑇𝑝𝑧𝑡subscript𝑇0𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬsuperscriptsubscript𝜏OL2superscriptℬ22superscript𝒜2erfℬsubscript𝜏OL2𝐴𝑡2subscript𝜏OL𝑧𝛿ℬ𝒜𝑡𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬ𝑧𝛿erf12subscript𝜏OL𝑡𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬsuperscriptsubscript𝜏OL2superscriptℬ22superscript𝒜2erfℬsubscript𝜏OL2𝐴𝑧𝛿ℬ𝒜𝑡\begin{split}T_{p}(z,t)=T_{0}+\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{% \sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\exp{\left(\frac{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}^{2}\mathcal{B}^{2}}{2% \mathcal{A}^{2}}\right)}\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{\mathcal{B}\tau_{\mathrm% {OL}}}{\sqrt{2}A}-\frac{t}{\sqrt{2}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}\right)\exp{\left(-\frac% {z}{\delta}\right)}\exp{\left(-\frac{\mathcal{B}}{\mathcal{A}}t\right)}\\ +\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\exp{\left% (-\frac{z}{\delta}\right)}\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}\tau_{% \mathrm{OL}}}t\right)\\ -\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\exp{\left% (\frac{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}^{2}\mathcal{B}^{2}}{2\mathcal{A}^{2}}\right)}% \operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{\mathcal{B}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}A}\right)% \exp{\left(-\frac{z}{\delta}\right)}\exp{\left(-\frac{\mathcal{B}}{\mathcal{A}% }t\right)},\end{split}start_ROW start_CELL italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ) = italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + divide start_ARG square-root start_ARG italic_π end_ARG caligraphic_C italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG caligraphic_B end_ARG roman_exp ( divide start_ARG italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT caligraphic_B start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG 2 caligraphic_A start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_ARG ) roman_erf ( divide start_ARG caligraphic_B italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_A end_ARG - divide start_ARG italic_t end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG ) roman_exp ( - divide start_ARG italic_z end_ARG start_ARG italic_δ end_ARG ) roman_exp ( - divide start_ARG caligraphic_B end_ARG start_ARG caligraphic_A end_ARG italic_t ) end_CELL end_ROW start_ROW start_CELL + divide start_ARG square-root start_ARG italic_π end_ARG caligraphic_C italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG caligraphic_B end_ARG roman_exp ( - divide start_ARG italic_z end_ARG start_ARG italic_δ end_ARG ) roman_erf ( divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG square-root start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_τ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_OL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_ARG italic_t ) roman_exp ( - divide start_ARG caligraphic_B end_ARG start_ARG caligraphic_A end_ARG italic_t ) , end_CELL end_ROW (S9)

where 𝒜=Ce⁢Cp/g𝒜subscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝𝑔\mathcal{A}=C_{e}C_{p}/gcaligraphic_A = italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT / italic_g, ℬ=Ce+Cpℬsubscript𝐶𝑒subscript𝐶𝑝\mathcal{B}=C_{e}+C_{p}caligraphic_B = italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT + italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and 𝒞=(1/δ)⁢Fabs𝒞1𝛿subscript𝐹abs\mathcal{C}=(1/\delta)F_{\mathrm{abs}}caligraphic_C = ( 1 / italic_δ ) italic_F start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_abs end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Substituting equation (S9) in equation (S7) we obtain the electron temperature Te⁢(z,t)subscript𝑇𝑒𝑧𝑡T_{e}(z,t)italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ):

Te⁢(z,t)=T0−π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢CpCe⁢exp⁡(τOL2⁢ℬ22⁢𝒜2)⁢erf⁡(ℬ⁢τOL2⁢A−t2⁢τOL)⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢exp⁡(−ℬ𝒜⁢t)+π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢erf⁡(12⁢τOL⁢t)+π⁢𝒞⁢τOL2⁢ℬ⁢CpCe⁢exp⁡(τOL2⁢ℬ22⁢𝒜2)⁢erf⁡(ℬ⁢τOL2⁢𝒜)⁢exp⁡(−zδ)⁢exp⁡(−ℬ𝒜⁢t).subscript𝑇𝑒𝑧𝑡subscript𝑇0𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬsubscript𝐶𝑝subscript𝐶𝑒superscriptsubscript𝜏OL2superscriptℬ22superscript𝒜2erfℬsubscript𝜏OL2𝐴𝑡2subscript𝜏OL𝑧𝛿ℬ𝒜𝑡𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬ𝑧𝛿erf12subscript𝜏OL𝑡𝜋𝒞subscript𝜏OL2ℬsubscript𝐶𝑝subscript𝐶𝑒superscriptsubscript𝜏OL2superscriptℬ22superscript𝒜2erfℬsubscript𝜏OL2𝒜𝑧𝛿ℬ𝒜𝑡\begin{split}T_{e}(z,t)=T_{0}-\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{% \sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\frac{C_{p}}{C_{e}}\exp{\left(\frac{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}^{2}% \mathcal{B}^{2}}{2\mathcal{A}^{2}}\right)}\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{% \mathcal{B}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}A}-\frac{t}{\sqrt{2}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}% \right)\exp{\left(-\frac{z}{\delta}\right)}\exp{\left(-\frac{\mathcal{B}}{% \mathcal{A}}t\right)}\\ +\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\exp{\left% (-\frac{z}{\delta}\right)}\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}\tau_{% \mathrm{OL}}}t\right)\\ +\frac{\sqrt{\pi}\mathcal{C}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt{2}\mathcal{B}}\frac{C_{p% }}{C_{e}}\exp{\left(\frac{\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}^{2}\mathcal{B}^{2}}{2\mathcal{A}^% {2}}\right)}\operatorname{erf}\left(\frac{\mathcal{B}\tau_{\mathrm{OL}}}{\sqrt% {2}\mathcal{A}}\right)\exp{\left(-\frac{z}{\delta}\right)}\exp{\left(-\frac{% \mathcal{B}}{\mathcal{A}}t\right)}.\end{split}start_ROW start_CELL italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_z , italic_t ) = italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT -
